title: dysoxylum malabaricum
intro: Dysoxylum malabaricum, also known as East Indian rosewood, is a plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae, native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Dysoxylum malabaricum include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and combat various health conditions.
